description: The figure presents scanning electron microscopy (SEM) images of Charpy V-notch (CVN) fracture surfaces from Fe-30Mn-9Al-2.7Si-0.9C steel (0.023% P) comparing the (a) as-hot-rolled condition versus (b) the hot-rolled, solution-treated (1100°C for 10 min), and water-quenched condition. Image (a) exhibits predominantly transgranular cleavage fracture morphology with visible {111} slip traces on cleavage facets, indicating brittle fracture behavior associated with κ-carbide precipitation and short-range order (SRO) formed during slow cooling after hot rolling, corresponding to low impact energy of approximately 23 J. In contrast, image (b) displays a completely ductile fracture surface characterized by extensive plastic deformation and dimpled rupture, resulting from the dissolution of deleterious κ-carbide precipitates during solution treatment, which restored high toughness (160 J). This comparison demonstrates that solution treatment transforms the fracture mode from brittle cleavage to ductile failure by suppressing precipitation-induced embrittlement.
